spec_match.rationale: Audits 12 at-rules with CSS.supports('at-rule(@x)') and gates a five-level progressive component on @layer, @container, @property, @scope, @starting-style. Exactly the spec's use case.

interactive.rationale: 12-row support table renders live and the level pips update based on probes, but CONTROLS_COUNT is 0 — there's no user-driven control to flip levels on/off, only what the browser reports. A 'force off' switch would let visitors explore the fallbacks.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index promises 'live audit of 12 at-rules … a card component that progressively enhances through 5 levels … green pips show which levels are active in your browser' — page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: Detects the case where CSS.supports('at-rule(...)') itself isn't supported (sets badges to '?' and shows a warning). Doesn't show what each level actually looks like when disabled — a side-by-side 'level N off' vs 'all on' rendering is missing.

references.rationale: HAS_CS_LINK is False. See-also links only to sibling concept pages, no direct https://chromestatus.com/feature/5110744177836032 link. CLAUDE.md rule #3 requires it on every concept page.

design_system.rationale: Raw hex colours throughout: #228b22, #9be0b4, #c0392b, #e6f4ea, #ffeef0. CLAUDE.md rule #5 says no raw hex — use --accent-emerald / --accent-rose tokens. The #228b22 dark green and the white-on-#9be0b4 pip would need contrast verification against AA at the same time.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus link to see-also
openQuestions.detail: CLAUDE.md rule #3: every concept must link to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5110744177836032 directly so the self-heal slug-recovery script can identify the feature. Add it to the see-also block.

openQuestions.title: Replace raw hex colours with design-system tokens
openQuestions.detail: Five raw hex colours (#228b22, #9be0b4, #c0392b, #e6f4ea, #ffeef0) appear in inline styles. Replace with var(--accent-emerald), var(--accent-rose), and either token-derived washes via colour-mix or new tinted tokens in public/styles.css. Verify WCAG AA on the badge text-on-background pair after switching.

openQuestions.title: Add a force-off control per level
openQuestions.detail: Visitors on Chrome 143+ see all five levels green and never see the fallback the page is designed to teach. A toggle per level that adds a class to opt-out of the gated CSS would let everyone explore the progressive degradation path.

openQuestions.title: Render the level count as a stepped progress bar
openQuestions.detail: '3 of 5 enhancement levels active' is a string; a five-segment progress bar with each segment labelled (and clickable to scroll to that level's row) would make the same data scannable and more in keeping with the rest of the showcase's editorial feel.

summary: Largest of the five concepts in scope but the weakest in design-system compliance — five raw hex colours and a missing chromestatus link. Once those land, add a per-level force-off so the fallback story is reachable on supporting browsers.
